svg.title: HND Health Social Care Evidence Framework: CASP Critical Appraisal RCT Qualitative, PICOS Framework, Evidence Hierarchy, Beattie Model Four Quadrants, Marmot Review Social Determinants, Ottawa Charter, NHS Leadership Model Transformational Servant Distributed, CQC Well-Led
svg.text: HND Health and Social Care: Evidence and Leadership Framework
CASP · PICOS · Evidence Hierarchy · Beattie (1991) · Marmot (2010) · NHS Leadership · CQC Well-Led
RESEARCH EVIDENCE APPRAISAL — Applied to Specific Studies, Not Just Described as Tools
PICOS FRAMEWORK
P — Population (who is studied)
I — Intervention (what is evaluated)
C — Comparison (vs what)
O — Outcome (what is measured)
S — Study design (eligible types)
Structures systematic literature search
EVIDENCE HIERARCHY (highest → lowest)
① Systematic review / meta-analysis
② Randomised controlled trial (RCT)
③ Cohort study (prospective follow-up)
④ Case-control study (retrospective)
⑤ Expert opinion / clinical guidance
Distinction: qualitative may outrank RCT for patient experience questions
CASP CRITICAL APPRAISAL
RCT checklist: randomisation, blinding,
follow-up, intention-to-treat, precision
Qualitative: reflexivity, credibility,
transferability
Internal validity: well-conducted?
Distinction: apply CASP to specific studies — what does quality mean for evidential weight?
HEALTH PROMOTION FRAMEWORKS — Beattie (1991) + Marmot (2010) + Ottawa Charter (1986)
BEATTIE'S MODEL (1991) — 4 Quadrants
Q1 Health Persuasion: Authoritative + Individual → mass media anti-smoking, leaflets
Q2 Legislative Action: Authoritative + Collective → smoking bans, sugar tax, food labelling
Q3 Personal Counselling: Negotiated + Individual → motivational interviewing, stop smoking support
Q4 Community Development: Negotiated + Collective → community health advocates, participatory
Distinction: evaluate equity implications — Q1 least effective with most disadvantaged populations
Synthesise Beattie + Marmot + Ottawa → coherent public health strategy recommendation
MARMOT REVIEW (2010) 6 DOMAINS
① Early childhood development · ② Education
③ Fair employment + good work · ④ Healthy living standard
⑤ Healthy + sustainable places · ⑥ Ill-health prevention
OTTAWA CHARTER (1986) 5 Action Areas:
Build healthy policy · Create supportive environments
Strengthen community action · Develop personal skills · Reorient health services
Distinction: evaluate which Marmot domains amenable to NHS action vs cross-government policy
LEADERSHIP FOR HEALTH AND SOCIAL CARE — NHS Management Critical Evaluation
LEADERSHIP THEORIES IN CARE CONTEXTS
Transformational (Bass, 1985): idealised influence, inspirational motivation,
intellectual stimulation, individualised consideration
Servant (Greenleaf, 1977): service to others as primary leadership purpose
Distributed leadership: shared across multi-professional NHS teams
Systems leadership (Senge, 1990): learning organisation — 5 disciplines
Distinction: evaluate NHS contextual constraints on transformational leadership
NHS LEADERSHIP MODEL + CQC WELL-LED
NHS Leadership Model 7 dimensions: inspiring shared purpose
leading with care · evaluating information · connecting our service
sharing the vision · engaging the team · holding to account
CQC Well-Led: vision + values · governance · leadership culture
staff engagement · learning from incidents
Distinction: evaluate specific service vs CQC criteria — not describe the framework
KEY LEGISLATION: Care Act 2014 · Mental Health Act 1983 (amended 2007) · MCA 2005 · HSCA 2012 · Equality Act 2010 · Human Rights Act 1998
Distinction: evaluate how legislation affects specific care management decisions — not identification only
